items.title: Allegations of double billing and charging for no service at Kalgoorlie hospital

children.content: A public hospital and private medical imaging provider have been billing Medicare for some services which are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

image.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

items.title: Could this famous architect-turned-priest be named WA's first saint?

children.content: The life of a world famous architect-turned-Catholic priest is being examined to decide if he should be named Western Australia's first saint. John Cyril Hawes constructed more than a dozen famous church buildings across regional WA.

image.altText: Image of John Hawes sitting in a collard coat and looking at the camera. Beside him is a small dog.

items.title: Farmers question plan to build youth centre near Ord cotton properties

children.content: The decision to locate Kununurra's new youth centre on an abandoned farm 7km from town has sparked local concerns.

gallery.altText: A grey shed surrounded by long grass and trees
A bale of cotton on farmland with red hills in background
An aerial of the Ord Irrigation Scheme

thumbnail.altText: A grey shed surrounded by long grass and trees
A bale of cotton on farmland with red hills in background
An aerial of the Ord Irrigation Scheme

items.title: Another pandemic 'something we need to prepare for', WA's public health chief says

children.content: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

gallery.altText: WA Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz speaks to a reporter

thumbnail.altText: WA Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz speaks to a reporter

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: NSW woman claims ignorance of plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of Australia

children.content: Fatma Skaf faced the Karratha Magistrates Court today over her alleged involvement in a plot to spirit Queensland bikie Dylan Cvek out of the country on a luxury yacht.

image.altText: A person wearing police/army uniform in a boat looking over at white yacht

items.title: WA man charged over alleged offensive emails to federal parliamentarian

children.content: The Australian Federal Police are linking a 70-year-old man to emails sent to an electorate office after an investigation that began in April.

image.altText: An Australian Federal Police shoulder Insignia on a Blue Uniform shirt

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

image.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: 30 years of Pokémon: The 'immersive' phenomenon still finding new fans

children.content: Three decades after the franchise that coined the phrase "Gotta catch 'em all!" was first released, parents are sharing their love of the franchise with their children.

image.altText: A man in a pokemon hat with his son with pokemon toys and photos

items.title: Biggest universal magnetic map unlocks new chapter of space research

children.content: Data gathered by scientists at the Square Kilometre Array has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

gallery.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

thumbnail.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

items.title: Australian composer cracks ABC Classic 100 top 35, but who will be #1?

children.content: Tens of thousands of Australians have cast their votes in the 2025 Classic 100: Greatest Of All Time. Join us as we count down to number one.

image.altText: Elena Kats-Chernin smiles wearing a black and white flowery dress, blue shawl and elaborate blue necklace.

items.title: Australia turns its attention to FIFA World Cup after practice games

children.content: The Socceroos have finished their warm-up games, and now turn their attention to Türkiye in their first World Cup match. So, what have we learned from their friendly games against Mexico and Switzerland?

image.altText: Cristian Volpato of Australia competes for the ball against Remo Freuler of Switzerland.

items.title: Russian teen Andreeva wins French Open to claim first major title

children.content: Mirra Andreeva defeats surprise finalist Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest French Open champion in more than three decades.

image.altText: Mirra Andreeva holds the French Open trophy.

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

image.altText: Port Adelaide players hug and and congratulate each other after a win

items.title: Bus driver may have had medical episode before crash that killed 61yo man

children.content: A passenger who was critically injured when a Transperth bus smashed into a tree in Perth's north dies in hospital, as police investigate whether the driver suffered a medical episode.

articles.title: Multiple people injured in bus crash, two critically, in Perth's north

children.content: A man and a woman suffer critical injuries and five other passengers are hurt as a Transperth bus crashes into a tree in Perth's north on Thursday morning.

image.altText: Police standing in front of a bus that is badly damaged after colliding with a tree

items.title: Will Australia be taken for a ride in the AI and data centre boom?

children.content: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: Testing underway after mass bird death in country WA

children.content: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a Wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.

gallery.altText: Two pink and grey galahs sit on yellow sunflowers.
Two galahs in a tree with a blue sky behind them. One is hanging upside down while the other appears to kiss it.

thumbnail.altText: Two pink and grey galahs sit on yellow sunflowers.
Two galahs in a tree with a blue sky behind them. One is hanging upside down while the other appears to kiss it.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

image.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? It's time to show off your skills

children.content: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Unlicensed chip factory lands WA's 'spud king' in hot water

children.content: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

A bowl of spuddies chips in front of two packets.
The entrance of the Spudshed outlet in Bunbury WA
